name: service-starter
summary: Backend / API service skeleton starter: a REAL, running std-only HTTP server exposing GET /healthz (config loading, standard logging, --version wired to build provenance), the release-automation starter pack's working package/release/deploy recipes, and a Dockerized-Postgres justfile/compose.yaml pair ready for when you add persistence.
post-apply-notes: REAL and RUNNING out of the box (Rust/std-only HTTP, matching the cli/gui/lib quick-start family's stack — no web framework dependency, mirroring the zero-dependency version_report.rs philosophy): `just run` starts an ACTUAL listening server; `just smoke` (or `curl http://localhost:3000/healthz`) returns a real `{status, version}` JSON body — the SAME contract the web quick-start template's `just smoke` already asserts. `-V`/`--version` prints the crate version plus, once packaged, the grimoire-build-info.json provenance stamp (§8); before a first `just package` it honestly reports a 'dev build' note. `src/config.rs`/`src/build_info.rs` are the SAME modules shipped in the cli/gui quick-start templates (self-contained per-template copies). SEAM: `src/http.rs`'s router is a real, minimal starting point (GET /healthz only) — replace with axum/actix/etc. as the service grows; `seed`/`migrate` stay informative placeholders and `run`/`test` do NOT depend on `db-up` (the healthz-only starter has no persistence wired yet — add the dependency back once you wire recordkeeper/a real DB). package/release/deploy/smoke are REAL, delegating to the release-automation starter pack's scripts/{package,release,deploy,stop}.sh (#431) via scripts/package-manifest.sh — `just package` and `just release` run with ZERO hand-written release code. A `justfile` + `compose.yaml` ship the idempotent, multi-worktree-safe Dockerized-Postgres `db-up` recipe (#81 — reuses a running shared container by exact name via `docker ps` filters, starts a stopped one, only `docker compose up`s when none exists; never Go-template `{{...}}` output which collides with just's `{{ }}`) with a container-level `healthcheck:` in `compose.yaml`, ready for when you add persistence; edit `db_name`/`container_name` at that point (the multi-worktree shared-container behaviour is documented inline near the DB recipes). Pick the language variant matching the project; quick-start-template applies that variant's test/build/lint/typecheck commands into the CLAUDE.md commands table + the v1.26 code-quality block (not edited inline) — kept for cross-profile consistency even though the shipped payload is concretely Rust. Config defaults are applied via model-effort-profile-switch + workflow-variant-switch.
